result.stdout:
Description:
  NSSharp — Objective-C header parser and C# binding generator

Usage:
  NSSharp [<files>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <files>  One or more Objective-C header files to parse.

Options:
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information
  --version             Show version information
  --xcframework         Path to an .xcframework bundle to discover and parse all headers.
  --slice               Select a specific xcframework slice (e.g. ios-arm64). Use --list-slices to see available slices.
  --list-slices         List available slices in the xcframework and exit.
  -o, --output          Write output to a file instead of stdout (or a directory when --split-by-header is used).
  --compact             Output compact JSON instead of pretty-printed.
  -f, --format          Output format: csharp (default) or json. [default: csharp]
  --extern-macros       Macro names to treat as extern (e.g. --extern-macros PSPDF_EXPORT MY_EXPORT). []
  --no-macro-heuristic  Disable automatic UPPER_SNAKE_CASE macro detection. By default, identifiers like NS_SWIFT_NAME or VENDOR_DEPRECATED are auto-skipped.
  --emit-c-bindings     Include C function declarations ([DllImport]) in C# binding output. Extern constants ([Field]) are always included.
  --split-by-header     For C# format, write one .cs file per input header (requires --output directory).
  --namespace           Namespace for generated C# output. Defaults to xcframework name when --xcframework is used.
